# servicenow_ui_script (Resource)

`servicenow_ui_script` manages a UI Script configuration within ServiceNow.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Manages a UI Script (client-side JavaScript) in ServiceNow.
resource "servicenow_ui_script" "example" {
  name   = "example_ui_script"
  script = "// example UI script\nconsole.log('hello');"
  active = true
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) Name of the UI Script.
- `script` (String) The JavaScript body of the UI Script.

### Optional

- `active` (Boolean) If set to 'true', this UI Script is enabled and available for use.
- `description` (String) Description of the UI Script.
- `scope` (String) Associates a resource to a specific application ID in ServiceNow.
- `type` (String) The UI type this script applies to. Valid values are 'all', 'desktop', or 'mobile'.

### Read-Only

- `api_name` (String) The scoped API name of the UI Script.
- `id` (String) The ID of this resource.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
# Import using the sys_id of the record in ServiceNow.
terraform import servicenow_ui_script.example <sys_id>
```
